task_completion.description: Locate the front_camera and velodyne_lidar in the Webots scene tree.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Webots GUI is open. The scene tree panel shows the 'autonomous_vehicle' node expanded, revealing the 'front_camera' and 'velodyne_lidar' child nodes.
The 'front_camera' node is selected in the scene tree. In the field editor panel, the 'width' field is set to 640 and the 'height' field is set to 480.

task_completion.description: Update the velodyne_lidar scanning layers to 16.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'velodyne_lidar' node is selected in the scene tree. In the field editor panel, the 'numberOfLayers' field is set to 16.

task_completion.description: Update the velodyne_lidar maximum range to 100 meters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'velodyne_lidar' node is selected in the scene tree. In the field editor panel, the 'maxRange' field is set to 100.

task_completion.description: Save the configured world file to the specified Desktop path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save World As...' dialog in Webots shows the file being saved as 'av_sensors_configured.wbt' in '/home/ga/Desktop/', or a file manager/terminal confirms the existence of this file on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Webots GUI to make changes rather than a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Webots application interface (scene tree, field editor) to modify parameters, not a text editor like nano, vim, or gedit editing the .wbt file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show commands like 'sed', 'awk', or 'echo' being used to directly find and replace text in the .wbt file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent saved the file from within Webots.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Webots 'File -> Save World As...' menu or dialog is visible in the screenshots, indicating a genuine save operation from the simulator rather than just copying/renaming the file in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target output path: /home/ga/Desktop/av_sensors_configured.wbt. Expected camera resolution: 640x480 (width=640, height=480). Expected lidar properties: 16 layers (numberOfLayers=16) and 100m max range (maxRange=100). The starting values were 128x64 for the camera and 4 layers / 20m range for the lidar. The robot name is 'autonomous_vehicle', camera name is 'front_camera', and lidar name is 'velodyne_lidar'.
